narrative: FY2024-25 shows a split benchmark. Jindal leads on absolute withdrawal and consumption due to smaller scale, and also leads on turnover intensity at 0.03587 L/₹. JSW is the best operational water-intensity performer at 2.369 kL/tcs and reports zero discharge, making it the strongest process-efficiency benchmark. Tata ranks 3rd on total withdrawal, 4th on consumption, 3rd on total discharge and 3rd on physical intensity. Tata’s 4.76 kL/tcs is about 101% above JSW and its 98.61 million kL consumption is about 584% above Jindal. SAIL becomes the absolute withdrawal laggard at 129.58 million kL and discharge laggard at 41.81 million kL, despite reporting significant ZLD actions including 128 million m³/year wastewater treatment/recirculation, new treatment capacity at Durgapur and lagoon desilting at Rourkela. Tata’s management priority should be reducing consumptive intensity, closing the gap to JSW, and converting ZED intent into measurable reductions in surface and third-party discharge.
